entries.title: Revenge Opens with Clone Wars Still Active

context.label: Episode III: Revenge of the Sith

content.fact: At the start of Revenge of the Sith, the Clone Wars are still active and Coruscant itself has been attacked.
content.injection: In Episode III: Revenge of the Sith, early ROTS scenes should still treat the galaxy as a Republic-Separatist war zone, not yet as a settled Empire.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episode III: Revenge of the Sith.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode III: Revenge of the Sith to Episode III: Revenge of the Sith.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

entries.title: Palpatine Publicly Appears as Captive Victim

context.label: Palpatine Rescued from Invisible Hand

content.fact: During the Invisible Hand crisis, Palpatine publicly appears as a kidnapped chancellor rather than as the hidden Sith architect.
content.injection: Keep Palpatine’s public role as victim and chancellor separate from his hidden orchestration unless the scene has Sith-side knowledge.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Palpatine Rescued from Invisible Hand.
Respect story-position bounds from Palpatine Rescued from Invisible Hand to Palpatine Rescued from Invisible Hand.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Anakin Gets Council Seat Without Master Rank

context.label: Episode III: Revenge of the Sith

content.fact: Anakin is placed on the Jedi Council but denied the rank of Master, worsening his resentment.
content.injection: During ROTS, Anakin can sit on the Council while still feeling humiliated and mistrusted because he is not granted Master rank.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episode III: Revenge of the Sith.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode III: Revenge of the Sith to Darth Vader Armored.

context.label: Episode III: Revenge of the Sith

content.fact: By ROTS, the Jedi Council distrusts Palpatine’s emergency powers and political control, but they do not initially know he is Sidious.
content.injection: Write Jedi suspicion of Palpatine as political and spiritual alarm before the Sith identity reveal, not as automatic full knowledge.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episode III: Revenge of the Sith.

entries.title: Darth Vader Name Applies After Fall

context.label: Anakin Falls to the Dark Side

content.fact: Palpatine names Anakin Darth Vader after Anakin’s fall to the dark side.
content.injection: Use Darth Vader for Anakin only after Sidious names him, unless a scene deliberately uses future knowledge or prophecy.

context.label: Order 66

content.fact: Order 66 turns clone forces against the Jedi and destroys the Jedi Order as a galactic institution.
content.injection: After Order 66, surviving Jedi should be isolated, hunted, or hidden; do not portray the prewar Jedi Order as still openly functioning.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Order 66.
Respect story-position bounds from Order 66 to Order 66.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Yoda and Obi-Wan Survive the Purge

context.label: Order 66

content.fact: Yoda and Obi-Wan survive Order 66 and become hidden survivors rather than active public leaders.
content.injection: After ROTS, Yoda and Obi-Wan should be written as hidden exiles or secret mentors, not open commanders of the Jedi Order.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Order 66.

entries.title: Padme Confronts Fallen Anakin on Mustafar

context.label: Anakin Falls to the Dark Side

content.fact: Padme confronts Anakin on Mustafar after his fall, and their relationship collapses under his violence and paranoia.
content.injection: In Anakin Falls to the Dark Side, on Mustafar, do not write Anakin and Padme as merely strained lovers; his fall has become dangerous and coercive.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Anakin Falls to the Dark Side.
Respect story-position bounds from Anakin Falls to the Dark Side to Anakin Falls to the Dark Side.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Luke and Leia Are Born in Secret

context.label: Padme Dies and the Twins Are Hidden

content.fact: Luke and Leia are born in secret as Anakin and Padme’s twins.
content.injection: After ROTS, Luke and Leia exist as hidden twins; ordinary characters should not know they are Vader’s children or each other’s siblings.

entries.title: Twins Split Between Tatooine and Alderaan

context.label: Padme Dies and the Twins Are Hidden

content.fact: Luke is hidden with the Lars family on Tatooine while Leia is adopted by Bail Organa and raised on Alderaan.
content.injection: After ROTS and before ANH, Luke belongs to the Tatooine farm context while Leia belongs to Alderaan’s royal/political context; their sibling link remains hidden.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Padme Dies and the Twins Are Hidden.

context.label: After Revenge of the Sith, before A New Hope

content.fact: After ROTS, Darth Vader’s identity as Anakin Skywalker is not public knowledge.
content.injection: In Imperial-era scenes, do not let ordinary Imperials, Rebels, or civilians know Vader was Anakin Skywalker unless source/context establishes that knowledge.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Revenge of the Sith, before A New Hope.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

entries.title: Jedi Order Destroyed but Not Erased

context.label: After Revenge of the Sith, before A New Hope

content.fact: After ROTS, the Jedi Order is destroyed as an institution, but memories, records, survivors, and Imperial propaganda still shape how people understand it.
content.injection: In Imperial-era scenes, portray the Jedi as purged and dangerous to discuss, not as a normal active public order.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Revenge of the Sith, before A New Hope.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
